content:
Meta has unveiled Muse Spark, a new multimodal reasoning model featuring thought compression and parallel agent architecture. Developed over nine months, it achieves strong performance with significantly less compute by rethinking training from the ground up.

• Rebuilt training pipeline delivers same capability with over 10x less compute than previous models, showing a fundamental advance in efficiency.  
• Large-scale reinforcement learning shows stable, log-linear improvement in performance as compute increases, with solid generalization.  
• Thought compression emerges during training: the model first thinks longer, then condenses reasoning, then expands again for higher performance.  
• Contemplating mode uses parallel agents to refine answers instead of longer chains, boosting performance without increasing latency.
